results.caveats:
The Ukrainian government has not fully implemented its new administrative structure reforms in the Autonomous Republic of Crimea [UA01] and Sevastopol [UA85]. Consequently, 31 ADM4 features within the Sevastopol ADM3 polygon retain P-codes that do not correctly conform to [UA85]. This will be rectified in a COD-AB update once the government has regained control of the Crimean Peninsula and fulfilled its administrative structure reform.
ADM3 Sevastopol [UA85] feature geometry does not align with ADM1, ADM2 and ADM4 Sevastopol [UA85] features.
31 ADM4 features within the ADM3 Sevastopol [UA85] feature have P-codes that are not aligned with the parent ADM3 feature. At the same time, they are aligned with ADM1 and ADM2 levels.
Names of some ADM3 features are still in the process of renaming by the Ukrainian government and are expected to be aligned with the respective ADM4 and ADM2 features in 2026. For consistency, new ADM3 names were incorporated into the dataset, while old names are still available in extra fields.
ADM4 layer does not cover the entire country. ADM4 layer represents settlement (populated place) areas only.

results.notes:
Ukraine administrative level 0-4 boundaries (COD-AB) dataset version 05.  
  
This dataset is structured into 4 levels:  
- Admin 1: 27 Oblast (Region)  
- Admin 2: 139 Raion (District)  
- Admin 3: 1769 Hromada (Community)  
- Admin 4: 29706 Settlement, partial coverage  
  
  
Note: This dataset is structured into 4 levels:
- Admin 1: 27 oblast (region) with three exceptions
- Admin 2: 139 raion (district) with three exceptions
- Admin 3: 1769 hromada (community) with three exceptions
- Admin 4: 29706 populated place (city, settlement or village), partial coverage  
  
  
Dates associated with this dataset:  
- 18 December 2025: dataset reviewed for accuracy and completeness  
- 01 September 2025: valid for use by the humanitarian community  
- 01 September 2025: last edit to the dataset before publishing  
  
Contributed by [OCHA Ukraine](https://data.humdata.org/organization/ocha-ukraine).  
Quality assured, configured, and published by [OCHA Field Information Services (FIS)](https://data.humdata.org/organization/ocha-fiss) and [HDX](https://data.humdata.org/organization/hdx).  
  
Part of the dataset: [Global Subnational Administrative Boundaries](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/cod-ab-global)

results.name: wfp-food-prices-for-ukraine
results.notes: This dataset contains Food Prices data for Ukraine, sourced from the World Food Programme Price Database. The World Food Programme Price Database covers foods such as maize, rice, beans, fish, and sugar for 98 countries and some 3000 markets. It is updated weekly but contains to a large extent monthly data. The data goes back as far as 1992 for a few countries, although many countries started reporting from 2003 or thereafter.

organization.name: wfp
organization.title: WFP - World Food Programme
organization.description: The World Food Programme (WFP) is the leading humanitarian organization saving lives and changing lives, **delivering food assistance in emergencies** and **working with communities to improve nutrition and build resilience**.

results.methodology_other:
Cohort component projection methodology applied to census, civil registration, and mobility data for ADM-1 geographic areas, then geographic disaggregation to ADM-2 areas using Iterative Proportional Fitting.

A technical accompanying note and an end-user release note (.pdf) have been prepared by UNFPA and will be provided with the data. These two notes describe the input data, estimation and projection methods, and strengths and limitations of the resulting COD-PS update. These notes are made available to end-users of the 2025 COD-PS update.
results.name: cod-ps-ukr
results.notes:
![UNFPA Logo](https://data.humdata.org/image/2021-08-05-143409.352392UNFPA-logo.jpg)

Subnational Population Statistics are produced by and contributed to HDX by [UNFPA](https://data.humdata.org/organization/unfpa) and, in the case of Ukraine, are exceptionally shared by OCHA Ukraine with the approval of UNFPA.

The 2025 update of the Common Operational Dataset on Population Statistics (COD-PS) in Ukraine draws on the best available population and demographic data (spanning 23 key data sources), as of July 15, 2025, and provides a snapshot of the projected population by sex and 5-year age groups at the ADM-2 (raion) level. It provides reference population estimates to guide humanitarian needs assessment and operational humanitarian response plans.

The 2025 COD-PS update was produced by UNFPA on behalf of the Humanitarian Country Team in Ukraine. The update comprises subnational population projections with a reference date of July 2025.

The updated COD-PS has been produced to support effective coordination of humanitarian and recovery planning, decision-making, and action in Ukraine. The disclosure and use of this COD-PS is for the members of the Humanitarian Country Team and those directly involved with recovery programme work in Ukraine. Until further notice, this data should be treated as ‘restricted’ and shared for this specified purpose.

The 2025 COD-PS update, like all COD-PS updates, is strictly a humanitarian data product of the United Nations aligned to the "best available humanitarian data standard". It does not represent or, in any way, replace official statistics or official population projections of the Government of Ukraine and it does not meet the international standards of official statistics.

results.caveats:
Border names are mostly from the State Border Guard Service of Ukraine, the State Customs Service of Ukraine, Wikipedia, and Ukraine 2026 COD-AB.

The types (Road, Pedestrian, and River) and statuses (Operational and Closed) of border crossings are based on the information from the interactive web map by the State Border Guard Service of Ukraine and the State Customs Service of Ukraine's web portal.

Note: These are aggregated data files organized by country-year and country-month. To access full event data, please [register](https://acleddata.com/register/) to use the [Data Export Tool](https://acleddata.com/data-export-tool/) and [API](https://acleddata.com/download/2833/) on the ACLED website.

organization.name: acled
organization.title: Armed Conflict Location & Event Data Project (ACLED)
organization.description:
[The Armed Conflict Location & Event Data Project (ACLED)](https://acleddata.com/"The Armed Conflict Location & Event Data Project (ACLED)") is a disaggregated data collection, analysis, and crisis mapping project. ACLED collects the dates, actors, locations, fatalities, and types of all reported political violence and protest events in more than [200 countries and territories in real time](https://acleddata.com/download/4404/ "[200 countries and territories in real time"). The ACLED team conducts analysis to describe, explore, and test conflict scenarios, and makes both [data](https://acleddata.com/data-export-tool/ "data") and [analysis](https://acleddata.com/analysis/"analysis") open for free use by the public.

ACLED is a registered non-profit organization with 501(c)(3) status in the United States. 

By using ACLED data you agree to abide by the [Terms of Use & Attribution Policy](https://acleddata.com/terms-of-use/"Terms of Use & Attribution Policy").

For questions or comments, please contact: admin@acleddata.com

Resources Available on the ACLED Website: The full disaggregated event-based dataset is available for download through ACLED’s [Data Export Tool](https://acleddata.com/data-export-tool/"Data Export Tool")  and [API](https://acleddata.com/download/2833/"API") with an [open access account](https://acleddata.com/register/"open access account"). Explore our latest data through our interactive [global dashboard](https://acleddata.com/dashboard/"global dashboard") and check in for weekly data updates and [analysis](https://acleddata.com/analysis/"analysis") tracking key political violence and protest trends around the world, as well as special projects like our [Early Warning Research Hub](https://acleddata.com/early-warning-research-hub/"Early Warning Research Hub"). Additional guides and methodology documents can be found in the ACLED [Resource Library](https://acleddata.com/resources/general-guides/"Resource Library").

Resources Available on HDX: ACLED makes a range of aggregated data files available through the HDX portal, updated weekly. Because the main ACLED dataset is disaggregated, ACLED data can be aggregated both spatially (e.g. to the national level) and temporally (e.g. to the month or year level), among other options. When a user downloads ACLED’s disaggregated event-based data, each row in the file represents a single event occurring between designated actors in a specific named location on a specific day. In the spatially and temporarily aggregated files available on HDX, each row in the data represents a country-year (e.g. Iraq-2017) or a country-month-year (e.g. Somalia-January 2010), depending on the file. Note: to access full event data, please [register](https://acleddata.com/register/"register") to use the [Data Export Tool](https://acleddata.com/data-export-tool/"Data Export Tool") and [API](https://acleddata.com/download/2833/"API") on the ACLED website.

resources.description: A weekly dataset providing the total number of reported political violence events and fatalities in Ukraine broken down by month. Political violence events include ACLED’s battles, violence against civilians, and explosions/remote violence event types, as well as the mob violence sub-event type of the riots event type.
